source_item.title: How a missed roommate interview helped Raj Subramaniam begin his journey to becoming FedEx CEO

slide1_hook.label: One Phone Call. Thirty Years. FedEx CEO.
slide1_hook.subhead: How a Kerala boy took his roommate's interview slot.

slide2_backstory.label: THE BACKSTORY
slide2_backstory.headline: IIT Grad With No Job Offer
slide2_backstory.body: Raj Subramaniam grew up in Kerala and studied engineering at IIT Bombay. He then earned degrees from Syracuse and UT Austin, but graduated into a brutal 1991 recession.

slide3_news.label: THE UPDATE
slide3_news.headline: Roommate Said No. Raj Said Yes.
slide3_news.body: In 1991, Raj overheard his roommate turn down a FedEx interview and grabbed the call himself. He got the job in Memphis, and in 2022 became only FedEx's second-ever CEO.

slide4_why.label: WHY IT MATTERS
slide4_why.headline: One Seized Moment Built a Global Empire
bullets: FedEx stock is up 102% since he took over
He now runs 500,000+ employees in 220+ countries
Credentials open doors, but boldness picks the right one

slide5_engage.label: YOUR TAKE
slide5_engage.headline: Raj had IIT and two US degrees, and still almost missed his shot. Is the real skill credentials, or the courage to chase your own opportunity?
slide5_engage.cta: Follow for more stories of Indian trailblazers reshaping global business. 🚀
